# Spec: feat(aws/managed_prefix_list): Add managed prefix list module
**Issue:** #236
**Status:** Spec approved — implementation complete in PR #237
**Owners:** @zachreborn @Jakeasaurus
**Type:** Feature

## 1. Background
AWS EC2 Managed Prefix Lists allow grouping CIDR blocks into a named, versioned object that can be referenced in security groups, route tables, and other AWS resources. Rather than repeating the same CIDR ranges across many rules, an operator references a single prefix list ID — updates to the list propagate automatically to all consumers.

There was no module in this repository for `aws_ec2_managed_prefix_list`. This spec covers the new `modules/aws/managed_prefix_list/` module.

## 2. Non-goals
- Managing `aws_ec2_managed_prefix_list_entry` standalone resources (the inline `entry` block is used instead, per AWS guidance for lists with ≤100 entries).
- Data source wrapper for AWS-managed prefix lists (e.g., `com.amazonaws.*` lists).
- Cross-region or RAM-shared prefix list support.

## 3. Affected module path(s)
- `modules/aws/managed_prefix_list/` (new)

## 4. Proposed design

### `variables.tf`
| Variable | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `address_family` | `string` | `"IPv4"` | IPv4 or IPv6 — validated via regex; forces new resource on change |
| `entries` | `list(object({ cidr=string, description=optional(string) }))` | `[]` | Inline CIDR entries via dynamic block |
| `max_entries` | `number` | `10` | Max capacity of the list; validated ≥ 1 |
| `name` | `string` | (required) | Prefix list name; must not start with `com.amazonaws` |
| `tags` | `map(any)` | standard defaults | Merged with `Name` key |

### `outputs.tf`
| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| `arn` | ARN of the prefix list |
| `id` | ID — used to reference the list in security groups, route tables, etc. |
| `owner_id` | AWS account ID that owns the prefix list |
| `version` | Latest version number of the prefix list |

### `main.tf`
- Single resource block: `aws_ec2_managed_prefix_list.this`
- `dynamic "entry"` block iterates `var.entries`; `description` uses `lookup` for optional field compatibility
- Tags merged with `merge(tomap({ Name = var.name }), var.tags)`
- No `count` or `for_each` at the resource level (each call creates exactly one prefix list)

## 5. Breaking-change assessment
- Breaking: **no**
- New module with no existing callers.

## 6. Checkov / tfsec considerations
- New suppressions: **none** — `aws_ec2_managed_prefix_list` has no Checkov checks that require suppression.
- Existing suppressions affected: **none**.

## 7. terraform-docs impact
`<!-- BEGIN_TF_DOCS -->` block in `modules/aws/managed_prefix_list/README.md` will be regenerated by the `build.yml` CI job on merge. Initial content is pre-filled manually.

## 8. Testing
- `tofu -chdir=modules/aws/managed_prefix_list init -backend=false && tofu -chdir=modules/aws/managed_prefix_list validate` — **passed**
- `tofu fmt -check -diff modules/aws/managed_prefix_list/` — **passed** (no diff)
- No apply test required (module library pattern; no live environment needed for validation).

## 9. Open questions
- None — resource is straightforward and well-documented.

## 10. Acceptance criteria
- [x] `modules/aws/managed_prefix_list/main.tf`, `variables.tf`, `outputs.tf`, `README.md` all exist
- [x] `tofu validate` passes
- [x] Dynamic `entry` block supports zero or more CIDR entries
- [x] `address_family` variable is validated to accept only `IPv4` or `IPv6`
- [x] All outputs exported: `arn`, `id`, `owner_id`, `version`
- [x] Tags merged with `Name` key following repo convention
- [x] README contains usage examples and terraform-docs markers
